content.problemStatement:
You are given a matrix AAA of non-negative integers with NNN rows (numbered 111 through NNN) and NNN columns (numbered 111 through NNN). For each valid iii and jjj, let's denote the element in the iii-th row and jjj-th column by Ai,jA_{i,j}Ai,j​.

You need to find a matrix BBB with N+1N+1N+1 rows and N+1N+1N+1 columns (numbered similarly) such that:


each element of this matrix is a digit between 000 and 999 (inclusive)
Ai,j=Bi,j+Bi+1,j+Bi,j+1+Bi+1,j+1A_{i,j} = B_{i,j}+B_{i+1,j}+B_{i,j+1}+B_{i+1,j+1}Ai,j​=Bi,j​+Bi+1,j​+Bi,j+1​+Bi+1,j+1​ for each valid i,ji,ji,j
The matrix AAA is chosen in such a way that at least one solution exists. If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one of them.
constraints: A matrix is guaranteed to admit at least one solution with 0 ≤ B[i][j] ≤ 999
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: the first line contains an integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 1000). The next N lines each contain N space‑separated non‑negative integers A[i][j] (0 ≤ A[i][j] ≤ 10^9).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output N+1 lines. Each line contains N+1 space‑separated integers B[i][j] (0 ≤ B[i][j] ≤ 999). The lines of different test cases are printed consecutively.

solution.approach: The relation A[i][j] = B[i][j] + B[i+1][j] + B[i][j+1] + B[i+1][j+1] can be used to compute the unknown B matrix row by row. Initialise the whole (N+1)×(N+1) matrix B with zeros. Then iterate i from 0 to N‑1 and j from 0 to N‑1 and compute B[i+1][j+1] = A[i][j] - B[i][j] - B[i+1][j] - B[i][j+1]. All three terms on the right‑hand side are already known because they belong to the current row or the previous row. The problem guarantees that the resulting value lies in the allowed range, so the constructed matrix is a valid answer. The algorithm is O(N²) per test case and uses O(N²) additional memory for the B matrix.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N²) for the B matrix (the A matrix can be read row by row if desired)
